Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

NASA’s X-59 Experimental Supersonic Aircraft Makes Second Flight

NASA’s quiet supersonic X-59 aircraft made its second flight on Friday, kicking off a series of dozens of test flights in 2026. Although the flight duration was abbreviated due to a technical issue, the team was able to collect information that will inform future tests. “Despite the early landing...

NASA's X-59 progress toward sonic boom mitigation through supersonic flight testing could reshape commercial supersonic aviation regulations and market viability.

See NASA →

British Airways Vs. Qantas Vs. Etihad: Which Carrier Wins Airbus A380 First Class?

Discover how British Airways, Qantas, and Etihad compete in A380 First Class, from privacy and luxury to dining and exclusive inflight suites.

Ultra-premium cabin differentiation on A380s is driving competitive advantage through bespoke suites and service experiences rather than aircraft capability alone.

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

NASA X-59 Second Flight Ends Early After In-Flight Warning

Test campaign set to continue soon despite in-flight issues.

NASA's X-59 experimental aircraft experienced in-flight anomalies requiring early mission termination, but the broader supersonic research program remains on track for continued testing.

Safety Mar 20, 2026

Press Release: STELIA to redefine Premium comfort at AIX 2026

Renowned globally as a leader in cabin interiors and a key player in Premium passenger seating, STELIA Aerospace is looking forward to exhibiting at the Aircraft Interiors Expo (AIX) 2026 in Hamburg, from 14 to 16 April. On an impressive 405 sq. metre stand (7A30, Hall B7), the brand will demonst...

STELIA Aerospace will showcase premium cabin interior innovations at AIX 2026, signaling continued investment in high-end passenger experience differentiation.

Business Aviation Mar 20, 2026

FlightPath3D’s strategic expansion into BizAv pays off

Though it has largely operated in stealth mode in business aviation for several years, FlightPath3D’s customizable moving maps with cinematic views are now flying on thousands of BizAv tails, and the Lake Forest, California-based firm is getting a little more vocal about its ever-growing footprin...

FlightPath3D's cinematic moving map technology has achieved significant market penetration in business aviation with thousands of aircraft deployments, validating its expansion strategy.

See Runway Girl Network →

Regulatory Mar 20, 2026

A photojournalist is challenging FAA’s drone ban over DHS vehicles

The FAA is being challenged on a recent flight restriction that banned drone flights over all government buildings and mobile assets. The lawsuit is being managed by The Reporters Committee for Freedom of the Press and is challenging it on both First Amendment and Administrative Procedure Act vio...

FAA's sweeping drone restrictions over DHS vehicles face legal challenge on constitutional grounds, potentially reshaping commercial drone journalism access.

Widebody Safe Haven? Airlines Forced To Ground & Store Aircraft As Iran Conflict Continues

Teruel is once again filling with grounded widebodies as Middle East disruption deepens.

Middle East geopolitical tensions are forcing airlines to park widebody aircraft in storage facilities like Teruel, signaling sustained demand uncertainty in long-haul markets.

Commercial Mar 20, 2026

The New Boarding Policy Changes That Southwest Passengers Should Know About

It comes after extra-legroom customers found that other passengers had already placed bags in the overhead bins above their seats.

Southwest's revised boarding policy addresses overhead bin conflicts, requiring airlines to better manage seat-to-bin assignment to protect premium-seat value.

International Mar 20, 2026

New York JFK's Highly Unusual New Long-Haul Airline: The Tiny Carrier You've Never Heard Of

A little-known Uzbek airline is set to launch highly unusual nonstop flights from Tashkent to New York JFK.

Uzbekistan Airways' new JFK service signals emerging markets' growing capacity to operate long-haul routes, expanding competition on transatlantic corridors.

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

Officina Stellare wins $2 million contract for lasercom ground station in Spain

MILAN – Officina Stellare, an Italian manufacturer of advanced opto-mechanical systems, has signed a 1.84 million euro ($2.0 million) contract with the Barcelona-based Institute of Photonic Sciences (ICFO), the company announced March 17. The contract covers the design and construction of an opti...

Optical laser communication ground stations represent emerging infrastructure for next-generation aerospace connectivity and satellite communications.

Rocket Lab launches eighth Synspective radar imaging satellite

Rocket Lab launched the latest in a series of satellites for Japanese radar imaging company Synspective on March 20. The post Rocket Lab launches eighth Synspective radar imaging satellite appeared first on SpaceNews.

Rocket Lab's recurring Synspective missions demonstrate sustained demand for dedicated small-lift launch services supporting commercial Earth observation constellations.

Industry Mar 20, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Integrating advanced technologies across airline, airport, and flight operations is essential to accommodate growing air traffic while meeting environmental sustainability goals.

See MDPI →

Military Mar 20, 2026

Talon IQ Testbed Performs Simulated Combat Maneuvers Controlled by Hivemind and Prism AIs

The Talon IQ testbed conducted combat air patrol and target engagement maneuvers controlled by ShieldAI’s Hivemind AI, before switching back to Northrop Grumman’s Prism AI. Northrop Grumman and Shield AI announced on Mar. 19, 2026, that the Talon IQ autonomy testbed aircraft, based on the Model 4...

AI-controlled autonomous aircraft demonstrating reliable handoffs between competing AI systems in combat scenarios signals maturation of autonomous military aviation capabilities.

London Heathrow dominates the US market in European aviation, with Charles de Gaulle and Frankfurt far behind.

London Heathrow's transatlantic dominance in 2026 reflects concentrated US connectivity that competitors must strategically address through route development and capacity optimization.

Historic Hangar One restoration completion preserves aviation heritage and infrastructure critical to Bay Area aerospace operations.

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

Another GPS launch shifts from ULA to SpaceX as Vulcan investigation continues

The change affects the GPS III SV-10 satellite, which had been slated to launch on ULA’s Vulcan Centaur rocket. The post Another GPS launch shifts from ULA to SpaceX as Vulcan investigation continues appeared first on SpaceNews.

SpaceX's Falcon Heavy gains critical GPS mission as ULA's Vulcan faces extended investigation, reshaping U.S. space launch competition.

Aerospace Mar 20, 2026

NASA’s X-59 cuts second test flight short after return-to-base call

NASA’s X-59 quiet supersonic demonstrator unexpectedly cut short its second test flight on March 20, 2026, after a return-to-base call. The… The post NASA’s X-59 cuts second test flight short after return-to-base call appeared first on AeroTime.

X-59's premature test termination highlights development challenges in quiet supersonic technology critical for future commercial supersonic certification.

Safety Mar 20, 2026

Delta Air Lines Grounds Airbus A319 After Cabin Loses Electrical Power Inflight

The flight made an emergency landing at Dallas/Fort Worth International Airport after the cabin temporarily lost power during its final approach.

Delta's A319 electrical failure mid-approach highlights critical need for robust power redundancy systems and thorough pre-flight diagnostics.

New report highlights pilot demand in business aviation and operator responses.

The Eurofighter Typhoon and Sukhoi Su-35 represent competing fourth-generation fighter philosophies, with their 2026 capabilities shaping NATO and Russian air superiority strategies.

Drones Mar 20, 2026

DJI Flip now cheaper than many beginner drones

There’s a reason drones often sit in online carts longer than they should. It’s not just about cost; it’s the friction. Too many controls. Too much setup. Too many things that can go wrong in the first five minutes. DJI Flip was designed to chip away at that hesitation. And now, with a fresh roun...

DJI Flip's aggressive pricing and simplified user experience are removing barriers to drone adoption by addressing setup friction and control complexity.

This NASA challenge tasks students to design innovative concepts that could further human life and work on the Moon, Mars, and beyon...

NASA's RASC-AL competition drives next-generation aerospace innovation by engaging 14 university teams in designing lunar, Martian, and deep-space concepts.

How Open NASA Data on Comet 3I/ATLAS Will Power Tomorrow’s Discoveries

The interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS will soon leave our solar system, never to return, but the observations of the comet will live on in NASA’s public data archives. More than a dozen NASA science missions turned their instruments to observe the comet, which is only the third identified object to be ...

NASA's open interstellar comet data democratizes space science research, enabling broader discovery innovation beyond institutional boundaries.
